mnemonics: OPTIMAL: Only Perfect Techniques Involve Mastering All Levels
keyPoints: Optimal data structures provide the best possible time or space complexity for a specific problem.
The choice of an optimal structure depends on the most frequent operations (e.g., insertion, deletion, search) required.
Analyzing the problem's constraints and access patterns is crucial for selecting the optimal data structure.
commonMistakes: Assuming a single data structure (like a hash table) is optimal for every scenario without analyzing the problem.
Over-optimizing for a rare operation while sacrificing performance for the most common ones.
Ignoring real-world constraints like cache performance or memory overhead when evaluating theoretical optimality.
